entities.name: SORGHUM MIDGE
entities.description: The pest Contarinia sorghicola Coq. that affects sorghum panicles

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The host crop affected by the sorghum midge

entities.name: VISUAL STIMULI
entities.description: Visual cues that influence the orientation behavior of the sorghum midge

entities.name: YELLOW RED AND WHITE COLOURS
entities.description: Colours that are attractive to the sorghum midge

entities.name: BLUE AND BLACK COLOURS
entities.description: Colours that are least attractive to the sorghum midge

entities.name: HALF-ANTHESIS
entities.description: The stage of panicle development where viable pollen and receptive stigmata are present

entities.name: VIABLE POLLEN
entities.description: Pollen that provides chemical stimuli for oviposition

entities.name: RECEPTIVE STIGMATA
entities.description: Stigmata that provide chemical stimuli for oviposition

entities.name: PHYSICAL REMOVAL OF ANTHERS AND STIGMATA
entities.description: The process of removing anthers and stigmata to reduce oviposition

entities.name: OVIPOSITION
entities.description: The process of laying eggs by the sorghum midge

entities.name: ADULT EMERGENCE
entities.description: The emergence of adult midges from the host plant

entities.name: MALE STERILE SORGHUM LINES
entities.description: Sorghum lines 2219A and 296A showing reduced oviposition and adult emergence

entities.name: ETHREL
entities.description: Chemical 2-Chloro ethyl-phosphonic acid used to induce male sterility

entities.name: SORGHUM CULTIVAR SWARNA
entities.description: A sorghum cultivar used to study chemically induced male sterility

entities.name: CHEMICAL STIMULI
entities.description: Stimuli from viable pollen and receptive stigmata governing oviposition

entities.name: PHYSICAL STIMULI
entities.description: Physical cues governing the oviposition behavior of the sorghum midge

entities.name: SORGHUM CULTIVARS IS 12573C S-GIRL-MR1 AND IS 2816C
entities.description: Cultivars showing antixenosis to adult midges

entities.name: ANTIXENOSIS
entities.description: A resistance mechanism where the pest is deterred from the host

entities.name: NO-CHOICE CONDITIONS
entities.description: Experimental conditions where the pest has only one host option

entities.name: SORGHUM CULTIVARS DJ 6514 AND IS 12666C
entities.description: Cultivars attractive to adult midges but showing antixenosis to oviposition

entities.name: SHORT FLORETS
entities.description: A morphological trait showing antixenosis for oviposition

entities.name: OVARY BREADTH
entities.description: A morphological trait negatively associated with oviposition

entities.name: ANTHER BREADTH
entities.description: A morphological trait negatively associated with oviposition

entities.name: TANNIN CONTENT OF GRAIN
entities.description: A chemical trait negatively associated with oviposition

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of the sorghum plant to withstand or deter the sorghum midge

relationships.source: PHYSICAL REMOVAL OF ANTHERS AND STIGMATA
SORGHUM CULTIVARS IS 12573C S-GIRL-MR1 AND IS 2816C
